div1.entryFree: Character, vb. (accented on the first or second syll.), to write, to inscribe: what's in the brain that ink may c. Sonn. 108, 1. thy gift, thy tables, are within my brain full --ed with lasting memory, 122, 2. in their barks my thoughts I'll c. As III, 2, 6. -- the light will show, --ed in my brow, the story of sweet chastity's decay, Lucr. 807. the table wherein all my thoughts are visibly --ed and engraved, Gentl. II, 7, 4. show me one scar --ed on thy skin, H6B III, 1, 300. these few precepts in thy memory see thou c. Hml. I, 3, 59.
